title: sterculia monosperma
intro: Sterculia monosperma, also known as the Katakand or Chironji tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. The tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The leaves, seeds, and bark of the Sterculia monosperma tree are used in traditional medicine to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the tree's parts are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of powders and capsules, to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
